data.narrative:
Mach Industries is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $185M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 8 private peers and 2 public companies operating in the same sector, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $185M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $352M. The peer-median method benchmarks against 8 same-sector, same-stage companies with valuations ranging from $300M to $1.5B; the median valuation across this cohort is $1.1B. The dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in the peer set—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI all trade near the high end at $1.4–$1.5B, while Castelion anchors the lower quartile at $1.0B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $299M to $1.3B, with a median of $738M. The wide spread reflects methodological tension: stage-progression conservatively extrapolates from capital deployed, while peer-median captures market prices for comparable risk and traction profiles. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Mach's operational metrics and market position align with higher-valued peers versus the broader cohort, and whether the stage-progression multiplier reflects sector-specific funding dynamics.
